profile.title: The Initiated Reader
profile.summary: Your instincts are already moving beyond surface symbolism. You understand that position shapes meaning, that cards in combination create narrative, and that the useful question is rarely what a card "means" in isolation.
profile.startPoint: Begin directly with spread practice. The Minor Arcana will be your focus — you don't need the Major Arcana foundations.

spread.name: Three Card
spread.description: Past, Present, Future — the spine of any reading.

card.name: The Magician
card.upright: All four suits appear on The Magician's table — cups, wands, swords, pentacles. He has everything he needs. One hand points up, one down: as above, so below. This is the card of directed will — of taking what you have and making something real from it. It asks: what are you capable of that you haven't yet attempted?
card.reversed: Skill used deceptively, or potential squandered. Reversed, The Magician often points to manipulation — someone using their abilities to mislead — or to the querent holding themselves back from their own power.
card.relational: Next to The High Priestess: knowledge and intuition working together, or in tension. Next to The Devil: skill turned to manipulation. Next to The Star: vision finally becoming action.

scenario.love: Someone showing their true capability — or concealing it. The early stage where impressions are everything.
scenario.work: A project where your full skill set is needed. Trust that you have what it takes.
scenario.self: The reminder that capability without application is just potential.

card.name: Temperance
card.upright: An angel pours water between two cups, perfectly. One foot on land, one in the water. Temperance is the art of the middle path — not as compromise but as mastery. In readings it speaks to the integration of opposing elements, to the kind of patience that is actually creation, to the alchemy of combining what doesn't obviously fit. What are you trying to force that would work better if you let it flow?
card.reversed: Imbalance, excess, impatience that is undoing careful work. Extremes where moderation would serve better.
card.relational: Next to The Devil: excess meeting its antidote. Next to The Lovers: values and action aligned at last. Next to The Hermit: inner integration through solitude.

scenario.love: A relationship finding its rhythm after conflict, or the need to temper strong feelings.
scenario.work: The long game — sustained, careful work rather than dramatic interventions.
scenario.self: Which part of you is being neglected in favour of another?

card.name: The Chariot
card.upright: Two sphinxes pull the chariot in opposite directions — one black, one white. The Charioteer holds no reins. He controls them through pure will and focused intention. This is the card of hard-won forward movement, of harnessing opposing forces and driving them toward a goal. Ambition, determination, and the refusal to be pulled apart.
card.reversed: Loss of direction, scattered energy, a vehicle with no driver. Reversed, The Chariot can also signal aggression used as a substitute for clarity.
card.relational: Next to Strength: willpower meeting patience — powerful. Next to The Moon: direction lost in confusion. Next to The Wheel: momentum working with or against you.

scenario.love: A relationship moving forward despite obstacles — or a person who is hard to keep up with.
scenario.work: Drive and ambition in full force. The ability to push through opposition.
scenario.self: What opposing forces in yourself are you trying to reconcile, and are you doing it with force or with mastery?
